channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Zuckerberg philanthropy ends DEI programs
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's philanthropy has shuttered its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs as well as work on immigration reform as it reduces social advocacy work, the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative (CZI) announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: This AI tool could help curb domestic violence
item.description: A new technology can pinpoint victims of intimate partner violence four years earlier than other detection systems and with 80% accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Sibani Ram, Fast Company

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google Pixel 10 model number leak hints that the next Google flagship is on the way
item.description: Model numbers spotted in an official database give us some idea about the phones Google is launching this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Coinbase lays out legislation wish list
item.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase laid out its policy vision Wednesday, urging Congress to pass legislation that gives clarity for the digital assets industry and protects consumers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing users' data with TikTok's ByteDance in another blow around privacy concerns
item.description: According to South Korea's data regulator, the two Chinese tech firms are "communicating." Here's what's at stake for users' privacy.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: 5 ways to fix OneDrive’s worst annoyances on Windows
item.description: On Windows, OneDrive is built right into the operating system. Even if you don’t turn it on, it will pop up occasionally, asking you to “back up your files to the cloud.” What that really means, of course, is that Microsoft wants you to move your folders to OneDrive. I’m not here to knock OneDrive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: More used Seagate HDDs disguised as new: Fraudsters make it tough to spot the difference
item.description: The controversy surrounding used Seagate hard drives has significantly widened, with fraudsters now targeting the company’s IronWolf and IronWolf Pro NAS drives alongside the previously affected Exos server-grade HDDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Black Ops 6 Season 2 Reloaded release time and what to expect
item.description: Here's what we know about the Black Ops 6 Season two Reloaded release time, including a look at the new skins and maps that are set to be added.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Más allá del ‘hype’: cuatro casos de uso de IA generativa que sí han funcionado
item.description: Si 2023 fue el año de la experimentación con la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa, 2024 fue cuando las empresas se centraron en casos de uso y comenzaron a poner en producción proyectos piloto.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intro to Elixir: A fresh take on functional programming
item.description: Elixir is one of the most interesting stories in the world of programming languages right now. Built on the Erlang ecosystem, Elixir offers a fresh take on functional programming and new ways of thinking about concurrency and fault tolerance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk faces more conflict-of-interest questions after DOGE fires FDA staff reviewing Neuralink
item.description: Since the moment the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) was first proposed, Elon Musk’s critics have warned that the world’s richest man was at risk of making decisions that could be a conflict of interest, given his multiple business operations.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: After leaving OpenAI, Mira Murati debuts her AI startup, Thinking Machines Lab
item.description: Thinking Machines Lab, an AI startup founded by former OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati, has tapped about 30 leading researchers and engineers from competitors such as OpenAI, Meta and Mistral, it said in a blog post on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Experts give up hope for 157 false killer whales stranded on a beach in Australia's Tasmania state
item.description: Marine experts have given up hope of rescuing more than 150 false killer whales that stranded on a remote beach on Australia’s island state of Tasmania.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Virginia Tech researchers raise red flags about mixed-reality security
item.description: In a new study, researchers at Virginia Tech spelled out a variety of security hazards that could compromise mixed-reality systems. The researchers highlighted security threats involving the manipulation of virtual objects when users collaborated via mixed reality headsets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EXL Code Harbor streamlines platform migration, data governance, and workflow assessment
item.description: With their outdated technology and high costs, legacy codebases hold enterprises back. But in many cases, the prospect of migrating to modern cloud native, open source languages1 seems even worse. Migration projects typically require significant time and money.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sea turtles return to the Atlantic Ocean off Florida after being stunned by the cold
item.description: Eight green sea turtles stunned by the cold during a rare snowfall in Florida have been released back into the Atlantic Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: These fantastic sites will save you from ever getting a bad seat again
item.description: When you’re booking travel, scoring a ticket to a sporting event, or securing yourself a spot at some other sort of show, you’re frequently faced with the impossible-seeming task of committing to a specific seat—on the spot. It may seem simple.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk doesn’t work for DOGE, says White House as battle for government servers intensifies
item.description: Attempts to challenge the power of Elon Musk and his DOGE team to close down government departments have hit an unexpected complication: according to the White House, the entrepreneur is not even in charge of the operation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Burchett compares DOGE staffers to young WWII soldiers
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Monday compared Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffers to young World War II soldiers. Burchett was pressed by CNN’s Pamela Brown on DOGE’s reported attempt to enter an IRS system, as well as its overall access to data.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: AI coding assistants limited but helpful, developers say
item.description: AI coding assistants still need to mature, but they are already helpful according to several attendees of a recent Silicon Valley event for software developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Juniper CEO: ‘I am disappointed and somewhat puzzled’ by DOJ merger rejection
item.description: Few would have predicted the U.S. Department of Justice would move to block HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. When it happened late last month, the two vendors and many industry watchers were caught off guard.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: JetBrains’ Ktor adds CLI for simpler project creation
item.description: JetBrains’ Ktor 3.1.0, an update to the Kotlin-based framework for asynchronous server-side and client-side applications, is now available, featuring a command-line tool for simpler creation of projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KREX 5
